custom.condition: A privacy or security incident is suspected or confirmed.
custom.reason: Potential legal, contractual, insurance, and regulatory clocks may already be running; intake must not delay immediate attorney attention.

custom.condition: A user asks whether the incident is legally a breach, whether notification is required, or whether an exception applies.
custom.reason: Reportability and notification are jurisdiction-specific legal conclusions that the workflow may frame only as questions for counsel.

custom.condition: A user asks for a notification due date, days remaining, or calculation of any regulatory, contractual, insurance, or data-subject notice period.
custom.reason: The workflow records user-supplied trigger dates and written notice periods but never computes or asserts a deadline.

custom.condition: The privilege posture or counsel direction for the investigation is unknown.
custom.reason: Distribution and investigation structure must be set by counsel before the working file is circulated beyond confirmed recipients.

execution_modes.purpose: Post the critical attorney-attention banner, capture the minimum incident and trigger-date facts, identify missing privilege information, and route immediately without reaching a reportability conclusion.

Build the privileged incident working file, question set, candidate notification inventory, contractual map, chronology, clock register, and preservation checklist without calculating deadlines or deciding notification duties.

Expand the standard working file across all provided contracts, incident reports, policies, affected populations, systems, evidence sources, insurance materials, and escalation factors while preserving the same legal-conclusion boundaries.

input_schema.label: Incident description
input_schema.description: What happened, how it was discovered, suspected vector, containment status, and facts known as of a stated time.

input_schema.label: Discovery and candidate trigger dates
input_schema.description: Each user-supplied occurrence, detection, discovery, confirmation, and vendor-notice date with source and ambiguity recorded; no due date is calculated.

input_schema.label: Privilege posture
input_schema.description: Whether counsel directs the investigation, who directs it, and confirmed distribution limits.

output_schema.label: Incident facts record
output_schema.description: A time-stamped provisional record in which every fact, estimate, vendor statement, and unknown is attributed and labeled.

output_schema.label: Candidate notification inventory
output_schema.description: Potential regulators, data subjects, counterparties, insurers, and other recipients framed only as attorney-confirmation questions with no asserted duty or computed due date.

output_schema.label: Trigger-date and clock register
output_schema.description: User-supplied trigger dates, candidate framework or contract, written notice period where provided, and a mandatory deadline-verification placeholder rather than a calculated due date.

output_schema.label: Working chronology
output_schema.description: A sourced chronology of occurrence, detection, discovery, escalation, containment, notices, and decisions without interpolated events.

output_schema.label: Evidence preservation checklist
output_schema.description: Potential evidence categories, owners, collection status, and at-risk sources for counsel to direct; not a substitute for a formal legal hold.

fields.description: The date and time at which an incident fact or estimate was recorded as current.
The counsel-confirmed privilege or distribution designation for the underlying material.
The person, vendor, report, system, or document to which the statement is attributed.

modules.load_when: a populated and attorney-approved privacy profile is available
counsel determines that a formal legal hold should be issued or managed
the incident chronology becomes extensive or litigation-grade
provided DPAs require substantive review beyond extraction of incident-notice language
